title: CSS Selectors, Tree Navigation, Cardinality, Text, and Attributes · Program Brief: Relative Component Extraction
summary: A brittle absolute selector returns the wrong repeated card, missing attributes become strings, nested text collapses, and a parser upgrade silently changes cardinality. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the relative component extraction increment in selector and extraction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Anchor extraction at a stable semantic component and resolve fields relative to it instead of coupling to page-wide positions.
State zero, one, optional, at-least-one, and many cardinality expectations and reject ambiguous or duplicate matches.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-relative-component-extraction — Anchor extraction at a stable semantic component and resolve fields relative to it instead of coupling to page-wide positions.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-selectors-tree-extraction-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-selectors-tree-extraction-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_selectors_tree_extraction_4(component_nodes: int = 8, extracted_records: int = 8, ambiguous_matches: int = 0, missing_fields: int = 3, provenance_nodes: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if component_nodes < 1 or extracted_records < 0:
        return False, extracted_records, "selector-count"
    if extracted_records != component_nodes:
        return False, extracted_records, "record-cardinality"
    if ambiguous_matches > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_matches, "ambiguous-selector"
    if missing_fields < 0 or provenance_nodes != extracted_records:
        return False, provenance_nodes, "field-provenance"
    assert extracted_records == component_nodes and ambiguous_matches == 0
    return True, extracted_records, "provenance-selector-contract"
